question:
Show that for every positive integer $n$,
\[
\left( \frac{2n-1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2n-1}{2}} < 1 \cdot 3 \cdot 5
\cdots (2n-1) < \left( \frac{2n+1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2n+1}{2}}.
\]

descriptive_long.question:
Show that for every positive integer $indexvalue$,\[
\left( \frac{2indexvalue-1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2indexvalue-1}{2}} < 1 \cdot 3 \cdot 5
\cdots (2indexvalue-1) < \left( \frac{2indexvalue+1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2indexvalue+1}{2}}.\]

descriptive_long_confusing.question:
Show that for every positive integer $algorithm$,
\[
\left( \frac{2algorithm-1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2algorithm-1}{2}} < 1 \cdot 3 \cdot 5
\cdots (2algorithm-1) < \left( \frac{2algorithm+1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2algorithm+1}{2}}.
\]

descriptive_long_misleading.question:
Show that for every positive integer $negfraction$,\[
\left( \frac{2negfraction-1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2negfraction-1}{2}} < 1 \cdot 3 \cdot 5
\cdots (2negfraction-1) < \left( \frac{2negfraction+1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2negfraction+1}{2}}.\]

garbled_string.question:
Show that for every positive integer $qzxwvtnp$,
\[
\left( \frac{2qzxwvtnp-1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2qzxwvtnp-1}{2}} < 1 \cdot 3 \cdot 5
\cdots (2qzxwvtnp-1) < \left( \frac{2qzxwvtnp+1}{e} \right)^{\frac{2qzxwvtnp+1}{2}}.
\]

kernel_variant.question:
Let
\[
P_n\;=\;1\cdot4\cdot7\cdots(3n-2)=\prod_{k=0}^{n-1}(3k+1),\qquad n\in\mathbb N.
\]
Show that for every positive integer $n$ one has
\[
\left(\frac{3n-2}{e}\right)^{\!\frac{3n-2}{3}}
\;<\;P_n\;<\;\left(\frac{3n+1}{e}\right)^{\!\frac{3n+1}{3}}.
\]
kernel_variant.solution:
1. Taking logarithms.  Put S_n = ln P_n = \sum _{k=0}^{n-1} ln(3k + 1).  The desired inequalities
   ((3n-2)/e)^{(3n-2)/3} < P_n < ((3n+1)/e)^{(3n+1)/3}
are equivalent to
   (3n-2)/3\cdot ln((3n-2)/e) < S_n < (3n+1)/3\cdot ln((3n+1)/e).

2. Estimate S_n by Riemann sums for \int ln x dx with step \Delta x=3.  Since ln x is increasing, on each interval [3k+1, 3k+4] we have
   3 ln(3k+1) \leq  \int _{3k+1}^{3k+4} ln x dx \leq  3 ln(3k+4).
Lower bound on S_n: summing the upper-integral inequalities for k=0,\ldots ,n-2 gives
   \int _{1}^{3n-2} ln x dx \leq  3 \sum _{k=0}^{n-2} ln(3k+4)
                     = 3 \sum _{j=1}^{n-1} ln(3j+1)
                     = 3 S_n.
Upper bound on S_n: summing the lower-integral inequalities for k=1,\ldots ,n-1 gives
   3 \sum _{k=1}^{n-1} ln(3k+1)
   = 3 S_n
   \leq  \int _{4}^{3n+1} ln x dx.
Hence
   \int _{1}^{3n-2} ln x dx \leq  3 S_n \leq  \int _{4}^{3n+1} ln x dx.

3. Evaluate the integrals (since \int ln x dx = x ln x - x + C):
   \int _{1}^{3n-2} ln x dx = (3n-2) ln(3n-2) - (3n-2) + 1,
   \int _{4}^{3n+1} ln x dx = (3n+1) ln(3n+1) - (3n+1) - (4 ln 4 - 4).
Divide by 3:
   [(3n-2) ln(3n-2) - (3n-2) + 1]/3 \leq  S_n \leq  [(3n+1) ln(3n+1) - (3n+1) - 4 ln 4 + 4]/3.

4. Exponentiation:
 Lower bound:
   P_n = e^{S_n}
       \geq  exp([(3n-2) ln(3n-2) - (3n-2) + 1]/3)
       = e^{1/3} igl((3n-2)/e igr)^{(3n-2)/3}
       >  igl((3n-2)/e igr)^{(3n-2)/3}.
 Upper bound:
   P_n \leq  exp([(3n+1) ln(3n+1) - (3n+1) - 4 ln 4 + 4]/3)
       =  igl((3n+1)/e igr)^{(3n+1)/3} igl(e/4 igr)^{4/3}.

5. Since e < 4 we have (e/4)^{4/3} < 1, hence
   P_n <  igl((3n+1)/e igr)^{(3n+1)/3}.

Combining with the lower bound gives the claimed
   ((3n-2)/e)^{(3n-2)/3} < P_n < ((3n+1)/e)^{(3n+1)/3}.

core_steps: Take natural logarithm so the product becomes a sum of logs
Bound that sum above and below by right- and left-Riemann sums of ln x using rectangles of fixed width 2
Evaluate the bounding integrals via ∫ln x dx = x ln x – x
Exponentiate the inequalities and take the appropriate (square-)root to undo the earlier factor 2
Insert the rough numerical bound 1 < e < 3 to tidy the end constants and obtain the stated double inequality

slot1.description: Common gap between successive factors / rectangle width ; determines the later square-root

slot2.description: Specific constant chosen as an upper bound for e when simplifying the right-hand inequality
